findings.description: u0003 (accessibility scale/contrast guidance plus GENERAL default render-size and stroke-color fallback) is not represented by any candidate rule; only u0001 and u0002 were clustered into rules, leaving this unit orphaned from the group.

payload.statement: Consider scale and contrast for low-vision audiences; default fallback is minimum 32x32px render size in email and Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds when unspecified.

findings.description: The GENERAL-tagged clause in u0003 functions as a default/fallback governance rule (applies when no other spec given) but this facet (default vs. mandatory) is not captured anywhere in the candidate rules, so no rule reflects its conditional/default nature.

payload.note: Applies only absent an explicit override; not a strict mandate.

findings.description: Source unit contains a compound statement: an explicit permission ('New icons are permitted') plus a hard constraint ('must match this stylistic approach'). The candidate rule captures only the constraint polarity (must) and drops the permissive scope, losing the allowance-with-condition relation.

payload.statement: New icons are permitted (may) provided they match the minimal line illustration style (must: consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, single-color line work).
